free_text: Spectral measurements of neutrons, protons, deuterons
and tritons produced by 100 MeV/nucleon He bombardment
To measure the energy of outgoing neutrons,
Heavy Ion Medical Accelerator in Chiba
Plastic scintillator (NE102A) was used as the
delta-E counter, ToF start signal detector, and
Liquid scintillator (NE213) was used as the E
The statistical uncertainties vary from 2-5% in the
low to medium energy (5-50 MeV) region of the spectra
and increase to about 30% at the highest energies.
Uncertainty in the number of accepted beam
particles incident on the target is estimated
Uncertainty in the solid angle for the
thickest carbon target, which has the maximum
efficiency is estimated to be 1%, considering
a small fraction of the slant incidence of a
